name: CLEAN SLATE CONSULTING
description_short: Clean Slate Consulting disputes negative items on credit reports and provides credit monitoring through a client portal with tiered service plans ranging from $99–$199/month.
description_long:
Clean Slate Consulting positions itself as a credit repair and financial empowerment company operating through an online platform. The company claims to work with credit bureaus and creditors to challenge negative items affecting credit scores, and offers 24/7 client portal access to track dispute progress. Their stated mission is three-fold: clean up past credit issues through disputes, track present improvements via live updates, and help clients maintain strong credit long-term.

The company offers three tiered service plans ($99, $149, $199 monthly) that differ primarily in the number of negative items disputed per month (15, 25, or unlimited). All plans include personal information disputes, 24/7 portal access, downgrade options as items reduce, and a 90-day money-back guarantee. They claim to address specific negative items including foreclosures, bankruptcies, collections, judgments, late payments, repossessions, and medical bills. They also advertise a "Credit Funding" service promising zero-percent interest rate funding for business, property, or personal ventures, though details are minimal.

Clean Slate distinguishes itself primarily through its guarantee structure (90-day money-back promise), personalized case analyst and advisor pairing, and the breadth of negative item categories listed. The website emphasizes education, transparency, and long-term financial literacy rather than quick fixes. Their referral program ($50 per friend) and emphasis on customized credit plans suggest a relationship-driven business model.

A significant caveat: the website makes broad claims about dispute success and zero-percent credit funding that lack substantiation, pricing details, or realistic outcome data. The "Credit Funding" offering in particular lacks clarity about terms, requirements, or actual availability. Like most credit repair companies, their core service—disputing inaccurate or outdated items—is legally available to consumers for free, meaning the value proposition depends entirely on execution quality and customer service, which cannot be assessed from the website alone.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

tiers.name: Credit Repair Program
features: Credit report analysis across all three bureaus
Dispute filing for inaccurate and unverifiable items

pros: 90-day money-back guarantee on all service tiers reduces financial risk
24/7 client portal access with live status updates on disputes and credit score improvements
Three tiered pricing options ($99–$199/month) to accommodate different dispute volumes
Personalized case analyst and advisor assigned to each client throughout the process
Addresses specific negative items including foreclosures, bankruptcies, collections, judgments, and medical bills
Plan downgrade option as disputed items reduce, potentially lowering monthly costs
Includes personal information disputes alongside credit report disputes
cons: Core service (disputing credit report errors) is available free to consumers under the FCRA, making paid service value unclear
Vague details on 'Credit Funding' service—no APR, terms, eligibility requirements, or realistic availability disclosed
No independent reviews, success rates, average score improvements, or dispute win rates provided on website
Monthly recurring fees ($99–$199) could exceed the cost of professional dispute services or DIY efforts for some consumers
Website contains unsubstantiated claims like '0% interest funding' and 'full money back guarantee' without clear conditions or fine print
best_for: Consumers with 15–25+ negative items who want professional dispute management and prefer not to DIY
Individuals seeking ongoing credit monitoring and accountability through a dedicated case advisor
People who value a money-back guarantee and prefer to outsource dispute communication with bureaus and creditors
diagnosis: Best for consumers overwhelmed by multiple negative credit items who want professional dispute management and ongoing accountability, though the lack of disclosed success rates and vague funding claims warrant caution. The core value depends on dispute execution quality rather than the service itself, which remains legally available for free to consumers. Consumers comparing credit repair companies should also consider whether credit monitoring services, secured credit cards, or credit counseling might address their needs alongside or instead of paid credit repair services.
services: Credit dispute filing on behalf of clients (up to 15, 25, or unlimited items monthly)
24/7 client portal with live dispute status tracking
Personalized case analyst and advisor assignment
Dispute item downgrade/plan reduction as items are removed
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 101 N Tryon St Suite 112, Charlotte, NC 28246
